# Platform Documentation Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Set up a Docusaurus documentation site at `apps/docs/` with auto-generated GraphQL/OpenAPI API references, developer guides, module docs, and package READMEs.

**Architecture:** Docusaurus 3.x app in the monorepo with two auto-generation plugins (`@graphql-markdown/docusaurus` for GraphQL schemas, `docusaurus-plugin-openapi-docs` for REST). Manual Markdown content for guides and module docs. READMEs in each package link to the site.

**Tech Stack:** Docusaurus 3.9.x, @graphql-markdown/docusaurus 1.30.x, docusaurus-plugin-openapi-docs 4.7.x, docusaurus-theme-openapi-docs, TypeScript, pnpm workspaces, Turborepo

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-16-platform-docs-design.md`

---

## File Structure

```
apps/docs/                          ← New Docusaurus app
├── package.json                    ← Dependencies, scripts
├── tsconfig.json                   ← TypeScript config
├── docusaurus.config.ts            ← Site config, navbar, plugins
├── sidebars.ts                     ← Sidebar navigation
├── static/
│   └── img/                        ← Logo, favicon
├── blog/
│   └── 2026-03-16-welcome.md       ← Initial blog post
├── docs/
│   ├── guides/
│   │   ├── intro.md                ← Introduction
│   │   ├── getting-started.md      ← Install, docker, first run
│   │   ├── architecture.md         ← Monorepo, modules, IoC, events
│   │   ├── creating-a-module.md    ← Step-by-step tutorial
│   │   └── conventions.md          ← Code style, testing, git
│   ├── modules/
│   │   ├── kit/
│   │   │   ├── overview.md
│   │   │   ├── database.md
│   │   │   ├── graphql.md
│   │   │   ├── ioc.md
│   │   │   └── event-bus.md
│   │   ├── auth/
│   │   │   ├── overview.md
│   │   │   ├── configuration.md
│   │   │   ├── permissions.md
│   │   │   └── apps.md
│   │   └── stock-location/
│   │       ├── overview.md
│   │       ├── mutations.md
│   │       └── events.md
│   └── api/
│       ├── graphql/                ← Auto-generated by @graphql-markdown
│       └── rest/                   ← Auto-generated by openapi-docs
├── openapi/
│   └── openapi.json                ← Static export of OpenAPI spec
└── src/
    └── css/
        └── custom.css              ← Theme overrides

packages/kit/README.md              ← New
packages/modules/auth/README.md     ← New
packages/modules/stock-location/README.md ← New
README.md                           ← Rewrite (root)
```

## Chunk 1: Scaffolding and Configuration

### Task 1: Scaffold Docusaurus app

**Files:**

- [ ] **Step 1: Create package.json**

```json
{
  "name": "@czo/docs",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "docusaurus start",
    "build": "docusaurus build",
    "serve": "docusaurus serve",
    "clear": "docusaurus clear",
    "generate": "pnpm generate:graphql",
    "generate:graphql": "graphql-markdown"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@docusaurus/core": "^3.9.2",
    "@docusaurus/preset-classic": "^3.9.2",
    "@graphql-markdown/docusaurus": "^1.30.3",
    "@graphql-tools/graphql-file-loader": "^8.0.0",
    "@graphql-tools/load-files": "^7.0.0",
    "clsx": "^2.1.0",
    "prism-react-renderer": "^2.4.1",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@docusaurus/module-type-aliases": "^3.9.2",
    "@docusaurus/tsconfig": "^3.9.2",
    "typescript": "^5.7.0"
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create tsconfig.json**

```json
{
  "extends": "@docusaurus/tsconfig",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "baseUrl": "."
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create docusaurus.config.ts**

```typescript
import type * as Preset from '@docusaurus/preset-classic'
import type { Config } from '@docusaurus/types'

const config: Config = {
  title: 'c-zo',
  tagline: 'Modular e-commerce platform',
  favicon: 'img/favicon.ico',
  url: 'https://docs.c-zo.dev',
  baseUrl: '/',
  organizationName: 'bnofao',
  projectName: 'c-zo',
  onBrokenLinks: 'throw',
  onBrokenMarkdownLinks: 'warn',

  i18n: {
    defaultLocale: 'en',
    locales: ['en'],
  },

  presets: [
    [
      'classic',
      {
        docs: {
          sidebarPath: './sidebars.ts',
          editUrl: 'https://github.com/bnofao/c-zo/tree/main/apps/docs/',
        },
        blog: {
          showReadingTime: true,
          editUrl: 'https://github.com/bnofao/c-zo/tree/main/apps/docs/',
        },
        theme: {
          customCss: './src/css/custom.css',
        },
      } satisfies Preset.Options,
    ],
  ],

  plugins: [
    [
      '@graphql-markdown/docusaurus',
      {
        schema: [
          '../../packages/kit/src/graphql/base-types.graphql',
          '../../packages/kit/src/graphql/filter-types.graphql',
          '../../packages/modules/*/src/graphql/schema/**/*.graphql',
        ],
        rootPath: './docs',
        baseURL: 'api/graphql',
        homepage: './docs/api/graphql/index.md',
        loaders: {
          GraphQLFileLoader: '@graphql-tools/graphql-file-loader',
        },
      },
    ],
  ],

  themeConfig: {
    navbar: {
      title: 'c-zo',
      items: [
        {
          type: 'docSidebar',
          sidebarId: 'guidesSidebar',
          position: 'left',
          label: 'Guides',
        },
        {
          type: 'docSidebar',
          sidebarId: 'modulesSidebar',
          position: 'left',
          label: 'Modules',
        },
        {
          type: 'docSidebar',
          sidebarId: 'apiSidebar',
          position: 'left',
          label: 'API Reference',
        },
        { to: '/blog', label: 'Blog', position: 'left' },
        {
          href: 'https://github.com/bnofao/c-zo',
          label: 'GitHub',
          position: 'right',
        },
      ],
    },
    footer: {
      style: 'dark',
      links: [
        {
          title: 'Docs',
          items: [
            { label: 'Guides', to: '/docs/guides/intro' },
            { label: 'Modules', to: '/docs/modules/kit/overview' },
            { label: 'API Reference', to: '/docs/api/graphql' },
          ],
        },
        {
          title: 'Community',
          items: [
            { label: 'GitHub', href: 'https://github.com/bnofao/c-zo' },
          ],
        },
      ],
      copyright: `Copyright © ${new Date().getFullYear()} c-zo. Built with Docusaurus.`,
    },
    prism: {
      additionalLanguages: ['bash', 'graphql', 'json', 'typescript'],
    },
  } satisfies Preset.ThemeConfig,
}

export default config
```

```

git commit -m "docs: add Turborepo integration for docs app"
```

## Chunk 2: Guide Content

### Task 3: Write Introduction and Getting Started guides

**Files:**
- Create: `apps/docs/docs/guides/intro.md`
- Create: `apps/docs/docs/guides/getting-started.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create intro.md**

```markdown
---
sidebar_position: 1
slug: /
---

# Introduction

c-zo is a modular e-commerce platform built with TypeScript. It provides a set of composable modules (authentication, stock locations, channels, etc.) that can be assembled into different vertical products: marketplaces, delivery platforms, ticketing systems, and more.

## Core Principles

- **Modular architecture** — Each domain is a standalone Nitro module with its own schema, service, GraphQL API, and events
- **Schema-first GraphQL** — `.graphql` files define the API, TypeScript types are auto-generated
- **Type-safe database** — Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL, migrations per module
- **Event-driven** — Modules communicate through typed lifecycle events
- **IoC container** — Services are registered and resolved via dependency injection

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Backend | [Nitro](https://nitro.build) |
| Frontend | [Next.js](https://nextjs.org) (React 19) |
| API | [GraphQL Yoga](https://the-guild.dev/graphql/yoga-server) |
| Database | PostgreSQL + [Drizzle ORM](https://orm.drizzle.team) |
| Monorepo | pnpm workspaces + [Turborepo](https://turbo.build) |

## Project Structure

```
apps/
  mazo/         # Nitro backend API server
  paiya/        # Next.js frontend (React 19)
  docs/         # Documentation site (Docusaurus)
packages/
  kit/          # Core toolkit (@czo/kit)
  modules/
    auth/       # Authentication & authorization
    stock-location/  # Physical inventory locations
  ui/           # Shared React components
```

## Next Steps

- [Getting Started](./getting-started) — Set up your development environment
- [Architecture](./architecture) — Understand how modules work
- [Creating a Module](./creating-a-module) — Build your first module
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create getting-started.md**

```markdown
---
sidebar_position: 2
---

# Getting Started

## Prerequisites

- Node.js 20+
- pnpm 10+
- Docker (for PostgreSQL)

## Clone and Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/bnofao/c-zo.git
cd c-zo
pnpm install
```

## Start the Database

```bash
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d
```

This starts PostgreSQL 17 on port 5432.

## Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file in the repository root:

```env
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/czo_dev
NODE_ENV=development
```

## Run Migrations

```bash
# Auth module
cd packages/modules/auth
pnpm migrate:latest

# Stock Location module
cd packages/modules/stock-location
pnpm migrate:latest
```

## Start Development

```bash
# From repository root
pnpm dev:mazo    # Backend API on http://localhost:4000
```

## Verify

Open the GraphQL playground:

```
http://localhost:4000/api/graphql
```

## Common Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `pnpm dev` | Run all apps in watch mode |
| `pnpm dev:mazo` | Run backend API only |
| `pnpm build` | Build all packages |
| `pnpm test` | Run tests with vitest |
| `pnpm lint` | Lint all packages |
| `pnpm check-types` | Type-check all packages |
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify docs render**

```bash
pnpm --filter @czo/docs dev
```

Navigate to `http://localhost:3000/docs/` — intro page should render.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add apps/docs/docs/guides/intro.md apps/docs/docs/guides/getting-started.md
git commit -m "docs: add introduction and getting started guides"
```

### Task 4: Write Architecture, Creating a Module, and Conventions guides

**Files:**
- Create: `apps/docs/docs/guides/architecture.md`
- Create: `apps/docs/docs/guides/creating-a-module.md`
- Create: `apps/docs/docs/guides/conventions.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create architecture.md**

Read these source files and synthesize into a single guide page:
- `CLAUDE.md` (Architecture section, Module System, Database Layer, GraphQL, Dependency Injection)
- `docs/planning/stock-location/trd.md` (sections 2-4 for data flow and component architecture)
- `.claude/rules/patterns.md` (Module Structure, GraphQL Codegen, IoC Service Registration)

The page MUST include these sections with `sidebar_position: 3`:
1. **Monorepo Layout** — explain `apps/` vs `packages/kit/` vs `packages/modules/` with the directory tree
2. **Module Anatomy** — `defineNitroModule` entry point, plugin lifecycle diagram: `czo:init` (register schema) → `czo:register` (access control) → `czo:boot` (services, GraphQL). Show the plugin code from `packages/modules/stock-location/src/plugins/index.ts` as example.
3. **Database Layer** — Drizzle schema definition, `SchemaRegistry` declaration merging, `Repository` base class (create/update/findFirst with soft delete and optimistic locking), `useDatabase()`, transactions. Reference `packages/kit/src/db/repository.ts`.
4. **GraphQL Layer** — Schema-first workflow: write `.graphql` → run codegen → implement resolvers. Show `registerTypeDefs`, `registerResolvers`, `registerContextFactory`. Reference `packages/modules/stock-location/codegen.ts`.
5. **IoC Container** — `useContainer()`, `ContainerBindings` declaration merging, `container.singleton()` / `container.make()`. Show service registration from a plugin.
6. **Event Bus** — `EventMap` declaration merging, `createDomainEvent`, `publishStockLocationEvent` as example. Naming convention: `module.domain.action`.
7. **Data Flow Diagram** — ASCII art: `Client GraphQL → Resolver → Service → Repository → PostgreSQL` + side arrow to `Event Bus`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Create creating-a-module.md**

Read `packages/modules/stock-location/` as the reference implementation. Write a step-by-step tutorial with `sidebar_position: 4` that walks through building a module from scratch. Each step MUST show the actual code from stock-location as example.

The 10 steps:
1. **Scaffold** — `package.json` (show exports map), `tsconfig.json`, `build.config.ts` — reference `packages/modules/stock-location/package.json`
2. **Database schema** — `src/database/schema.ts` (show `stockLocations` table definition), `src/database/relations.ts` — reference `packages/modules/stock-location/src/database/schema.ts`
3. **Migration** — `drizzle.config.ts`, `pnpm migrate:create`, show migration SQL — reference `packages/modules/stock-location/migrations/0001_create_stock_locations.sql`
4. **Service** — `src/services/stock-location.service.ts` (show factory pattern, Zod validation, Repository usage) — reference the `create()` function
5. **Events** — `src/events/types.ts` (show `STOCK_LOCATION_EVENTS` map and `EventMap` merging), `src/events/stock-location-events.ts` (show `publishStockLocationEvent`)
6. **GraphQL schema** — `src/graphql/schema/stock-location/schema.graphql` (show types + mutation), `codegen.ts` config, run `pnpm generate`
7. **Resolvers** — show `createStockLocation.ts` resolver calling `ctx.stockLocation.service.create()`
8. **Type declarations** — `src/types.ts` (show `SchemaRegistry`, `ContainerBindings`, `GraphQLContextMap` merging)
9. **Plugin** — `src/plugins/index.ts` (show all 3 lifecycle hooks)
10. **Module entry** — `src/module.ts` (show `defineNitroModule`), register in `apps/mazo/nitro.config.ts`

- [ ] **Step 3: Create conventions.md**

Read these source files and synthesize into a single conventions page with `sidebar_position: 5`:
- `.claude/rules/coding-style.md`
- `.claude/rules/testing.md`
- `.claude/rules/git-workflow.md`
- `.claude/rules/security.md`

The page MUST include these sections:
1. **Coding Style** — Immutability (spread, not assignment), file size limits (200-400 lines, 800 max), function limits (50 lines, 4 levels nesting), no console.log, Zod at system boundaries, soft deletion (`deletedAt`), optimistic locking (`version`)
2. **Testing** — 80% minimum coverage, TDD workflow (RED → GREEN → IMPROVE), vitest, test types (unit, integration, E2E)
3. **Git Workflow** — Conventional commits (`feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, etc.), PR workflow (analyze changes, draft summary, test plan)
4. **Security** — No hardcoded secrets, parameterized queries (Drizzle handles this), no internal details in error messages, validate all user input

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify all guide pages render with correct sidebar**

```bash
pnpm --filter @czo/docs dev
```

Navigate through each guide page.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add apps/docs/docs/guides/
git commit -m "docs: add architecture, creating a module, and conventions guides"
```

## Chunk 3: Module Documentation

### Task 5: Write Kit module docs

**Files:**
- Create: `apps/docs/docs/modules/kit/overview.md`
- Create: `apps/docs/docs/modules/kit/database.md`
- Create: `apps/docs/docs/modules/kit/graphql.md`
- Create: `apps/docs/docs/modules/kit/ioc.md`
- Create: `apps/docs/docs/modules/kit/event-bus.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create overview.md**

Read `packages/kit/package.json` (exports map) and `packages/kit/src/index.ts`. Write a page with `sidebar_position: 1` that includes:
- One-paragraph description: core toolkit that provides database, GraphQL, IoC, event bus, and module system abstractions
- **Subpath Exports table** — list each export (`@czo/kit`, `@czo/kit/db`, `@czo/kit/graphql`, `@czo/kit/ioc`, `@czo/kit/event-bus`, `@czo/kit/nitro`, `@czo/kit/cache`, `@czo/kit/queue`, `@czo/kit/telemetry`) with a one-line description
- **How modules depend on it** — diagram showing kit at the center with auth and stock-location depending on it

- [ ] **Step 2: Create database.md**

Read `packages/kit/src/db/repository.ts` and `packages/kit/src/db/index.ts`. Write a page with `sidebar_position: 2` that includes:
- **Schema Definition** — show how to define a Drizzle table with `pgTable()`, include `deletedAt` and `version` columns. Show `SchemaRegistry` declaration merging.
- **Repository Class** — document the public API: `create(value, opts?)`, `update(value, opts?)`, `findFirst(config)`, `findMany(config)`. Show the `expectedVersion` option for optimistic locking. Show `beforeCreate`/`afterCreate` hooks.
- **useDatabase()** — how to get a typed DB instance
- **Transactions** — `db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })` with `{ tx: tx as any }` passed to repo methods
- **Migrations** — `drizzle-kit` commands: `pnpm migrate:create <name>`, `pnpm migrate:latest`, `pnpm migrate:status`

- [ ] **Step 3: Create graphql.md**

Read `packages/kit/src/graphql/` and `packages/modules/stock-location/codegen.ts`. Write a page with `sidebar_position: 3` that includes:
- **Schema-First Workflow** — write `.graphql` → configure `codegen.ts` → run `pnpm generate` → implement resolvers
- **Registration Functions** — `registerTypeDefs(typeDefs)`, `registerResolvers(resolvers)`, `registerContextFactory(name, factory)` with code examples from stock-location
- **GraphQLContextMap** — how to declare module context via declaration merging in `src/types.ts`
- **Codegen Config** — walk through `codegen.ts` with `@eddeee888/gcg-typescript-resolver-files`, explain `mappers`, `scalarsOverrides`, `externalResolvers`

- [ ] **Step 4: Create ioc.md**

Read `packages/kit/src/ioc/`. Write a page with `sidebar_position: 4` that includes:
- **Container Access** — `useContainer()` returns the `@adonisjs/fold` container
- **Binding Services** — `container.singleton('key', () => service)` in plugin `czo:boot` hook
- **Resolving Services** — `await container.make('key')` in context factories and other plugins
- **Type Safety** — `ContainerBindings` interface declaration merging in `src/types.ts`, show example
- **Lifecycle** — services are only available after `czo:boot`, not during `czo:init`

- [ ] **Step 5: Create event-bus.md**

Read `packages/kit/src/event-bus/` and `packages/modules/stock-location/src/events/`. Write a page with `sidebar_position: 5` that includes:
- **EventMap Declaration** — show how stock-location declares its events via `declare module '@czo/kit/event-bus'`
- **Event Payloads** — show `StockLocationCreatedPayload` and `StockLocationUpdatedPayload` as examples
- **Publishing Events** — show `publishStockLocationEvent()` wrapper: fire-and-forget with catch-and-warn
- **Naming Convention** — `module.domain.action` (e.g., `stockLocation.location.created`)
- **Subscribing** — how consumers can listen to events (future: via event bus subscriptions)

- Create: `apps/docs/docs/modules/stock-location/events.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write Auth module pages**

Read `packages/modules/auth/src/` and `docs/planning/auth/`. Follow the Module Documentation Template from the spec (Overview, Installation, Configuration, Usage, Database Schema, Events, Permissions). Create 4 pages:

- `overview.md` (sidebar_position: 1) — What auth provides (session-based auth via better-auth, organization-scoped permissions, app system). Installation steps (add to `nitro.config.ts`, run migration). Database tables overview. Read `packages/modules/auth/src/database/schema.ts` for table list.
- `configuration.md` (sidebar_position: 2) — Environment variables (`NITRO_CZO_AUTH_SECRET`). Auth options from `packages/modules/auth/src/config/`. Better-auth integration.
- `permissions.md` (sidebar_position: 3) — Access control model: resources, actions, `@permission` GraphQL directive. Role hierarchy (member → manager → owner). How modules register their access domain (show stock-location's `accessService.register()` as example). Read `packages/modules/auth/src/services/`.
- `apps.md` (sidebar_position: 4) — App install lifecycle: manifest schema, `installApp` mutation, webhooks, API keys, `uninstallApp`. Read `packages/modules/auth/src/services/app.service.ts` and `packages/modules/auth/src/graphql/schema/app/schema.graphql`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Write Stock Location module pages**

Read `packages/modules/stock-location/src/` and `docs/planning/stock-location/trd.md`. Follow the Module Documentation Template. Create 3 pages:

- `overview.md` (sidebar_position: 1) — What it manages (warehouses, stores, fulfillment centers). Two tables: `stock_locations` + `stock_location_addresses` (1:1). Key features: auto-generated handles via slugify, soft delete, nested address input. Installation steps. Database schema table (columns, types, constraints from `src/database/schema.ts`). Permissions (resource: `stock-location`, actions: create/read/update/delete, hierarchy: member/manager/owner).
- `mutations.md` (sidebar_position: 2) — Document each mutation with GraphQL examples:
  - `createStockLocation(input: CreateStockLocationInput!)` — show example with nested address, explain handle auto-generation
  - `updateStockLocation(id: ID!, input: UpdateStockLocationInput!)` — show example with and without address, explain patch semantics
  - `updateStockLocationAddress(stockLocationId: ID!, input: UpdateStockLocationAddressInput!)` — show example, explain null-clearing for nullable fields
  Read `src/graphql/schema/stock-location/schema.graphql` for exact input types.
- `events.md` (sidebar_position: 3) — Document each event with TypeScript payload interface:
  - `stockLocation.location.created` — `{ id, organizationId, handle, name }`
  - `stockLocation.location.updated` — `{ id, organizationId, changes: string[] }` — explain that `changes` lists field names from input (intent-based)
  Read `src/events/types.ts` for all event types.

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify module pages render**

```bash
pnpm --filter @czo/docs dev
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add apps/docs/docs/modules/
git commit -m "docs: add Auth and Stock Location module documentation"
```

## Chunk 4: API Reference and Auto-Generation

### Task 7: Configure and run GraphQL auto-generation

**Files:**
- Modify: `apps/docs/docusaurus.config.ts` (already configured in Task 1)

- [ ] **Step 1: Create GraphQL API index page**

```markdown
---
id: index
title: GraphQL API Reference
sidebar_position: 1
---

# GraphQL API Reference

This reference is auto-generated from the GraphQL schema files in the codebase.

## Endpoint

```
POST /api/graphql
```

## Authentication

All requests require a valid session. See [Auth module](/docs/modules/auth/overview) for details.
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run GraphQL generation**

```bash
cd apps/docs && pnpm generate:graphql
```

Expected: Markdown files generated in `apps/docs/docs/api/graphql/`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify generated pages render**

```bash
pnpm --filter @czo/docs dev
```

Navigate to API Reference → GraphQL in the sidebar.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit generated files**

```bash

git commit -m "docs: add auto-generated GraphQL API reference"

### Task 8: Export and configure OpenAPI docs

**Files:**
- Create: `apps/docs/openapi/openapi.json`
- Modify: `apps/docs/package.json` (add openapi plugin deps)
- Modify: `apps/docs/docusaurus.config.ts` (add openapi plugin)

- [ ] **Step 1: Export OpenAPI spec to static file**

Start the backend and fetch the spec:

```bash
# In another terminal: pnpm dev:mazo
curl http://localhost:4000/api/_nitro/openapi.json > apps/docs/openapi/openapi.json
```

If the spec is incomplete or the server isn't available, create a minimal placeholder:

```json
{
  "openapi": "3.0.0",
  "info": { "title": "c-zo API", "version": "0.1.0" },
  "paths": {}
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add OpenAPI plugin dependencies**

Add to `apps/docs/package.json`:
```json
"docusaurus-plugin-openapi-docs": "^4.7.1",
"docusaurus-theme-openapi-docs": "^4.7.1"
```

Run `pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Update docusaurus.config.ts**

Add to `plugins` array:
```typescript
[
  'docusaurus-plugin-openapi-docs',
  {
    id: 'openapi',
    docsPluginId: 'classic',
    config: {
      api: {
        specPath: './openapi/openapi.json',
        outputDir: './docs/api/rest',
      },
    },
  },
],
```

Add to `themes` array:
```typescript
themes: ['docusaurus-theme-openapi-docs'],
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Generate OpenAPI docs**

```bash
cd apps/docs && pnpm docusaurus gen-api-docs all
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Update generate script**

Update `apps/docs/package.json` scripts:
```json
"generate": "pnpm generate:graphql && pnpm generate:openapi",
"generate:openapi": "docusaurus gen-api-docs all"
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Verify REST API pages render**

```bash
pnpm --filter @czo/docs dev
```

- [ ] **Step 7: Commit**

```bash
git add apps/docs/openapi/ apps/docs/docs/api/rest/ apps/docs/package.json apps/docs/docusaurus.config.ts pnpm-lock.yaml
git commit -m "docs: add OpenAPI plugin and REST API reference"
```

## Chunk 5: READMEs and Root README

### Task 9: Write package READMEs

**Files:**
- Create: `packages/kit/README.md`
- Create: `packages/modules/auth/README.md`
- Create: `packages/modules/stock-location/README.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create Kit README**

```markdown
# @czo/kit

Core toolkit for the c-zo modular e-commerce platform.

## Quick Start

`@czo/kit` is included automatically as a dependency of all c-zo modules. You don't install it directly.

## Subpath Exports

| Export | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `@czo/kit` | Logger, core types |
| `@czo/kit/db` | Drizzle ORM, Repository base class, migrations |
| `@czo/kit/graphql` | Schema registration, context factories, codegen helpers |
| `@czo/kit/ioc` | IoC container, `useContainer()` |
| `@czo/kit/event-bus` | Typed event bus, domain events |
| `@czo/kit/nitro` | `defineNitroModule`, plugin helpers |
| `@czo/kit/cache` | Cache utilities |
| `@czo/kit/queue` | Queue abstraction |
| `@czo/kit/telemetry` | Telemetry/observability |

## Documentation

Full docs: https://docs.c-zo.dev/docs/modules/kit/overview
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create Auth README**

```markdown
# @czo/auth

Authentication, authorization, and app management for the c-zo platform.

## Quick Start

```typescript
// apps/mazo/nitro.config.ts
modules: ['@czo/auth', kitModule]
```

```bash
cd packages/modules/auth
pnpm migrate:latest
```

## Key Concepts

- **Session-based auth** via better-auth
- **Organization-scoped permissions** with resource/action model
- **Role hierarchy** (member → manager → owner)
- **App system** with manifest-based install, webhooks, and API keys

## API

| Operation | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Organization CRUD | Mutations | Create, update, manage organizations |
| User management | Queries/Mutations | Users, sessions, invitations |
| App install/uninstall | Mutations | Install third-party apps |
| Two-factor auth | Mutations | TOTP setup and verification |

## Documentation

Full docs: https://docs.c-zo.dev/docs/modules/auth/overview
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create Stock Location README**

```markdown
# @czo/stock-location

Stock location management for the c-zo platform. Manages physical inventory locations (warehouses, stores, fulfillment centers).

## Quick Start

```typescript
// apps/mazo/nitro.config.ts
modules: ['@czo/auth', '@czo/stock-location', kitModule]
```

```bash
cd packages/modules/stock-location
pnpm migrate:latest
```

## Key Concepts

- **Auto-generated handles** — URL-safe slugs from location names
- **1:1 address relation** — Each location has exactly one address
- **Soft delete** — Locations are never hard-deleted (`deletedAt` field)
- **Lifecycle events** — `stockLocation.location.created`, `stockLocation.location.updated`

## API

| Operation | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `createStockLocation` | Mutation | Create a location with address |
| `updateStockLocation` | Mutation | Update name, handle, metadata, and/or address |
| `updateStockLocationAddress` | Mutation | Update address fields independently |

## Documentation

Full docs: https://docs.c-zo.dev/docs/modules/stock-location/overview
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add packages/kit/README.md packages/modules/auth/README.md packages/modules/stock-location/README.md
git commit -m "docs: add package READMEs for kit, auth, and stock-location"
```

---

### Task 10: Rewrite root README

**Files:**
- Modify: `README.md` (root)

- [ ] **Step 1: Rewrite README.md**

```markdown
# c-zo

Modular e-commerce platform built with TypeScript, Nitro, and GraphQL.

c-zo provides composable modules that can be assembled into different vertical products: marketplaces, delivery platforms, ticketing systems, and more.

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone https://github.com/bnofao/c-zo.git
cd c-zo
pnpm install

# Start PostgreSQL
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d

# Run migrations
cd packages/modules/auth && pnpm migrate:latest
cd ../stock-location && pnpm migrate:latest

# Start development
cd ../../..
pnpm dev:mazo
```

API available at `http://localhost:4000/api/graphql`.

## Architecture

```
apps/
  mazo/               # Nitro backend API server
  paiya/              # Next.js frontend (React 19)
  docs/               # Documentation site (Docusaurus)
packages/
  kit/                # Core toolkit (@czo/kit)
  modules/
    auth/             # Authentication & authorization
    stock-location/   # Physical inventory locations
  ui/                 # Shared React components
```

## Modules

| Module | Package | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| Kit | [`@czo/kit`](packages/kit) | Core toolkit: database, GraphQL, IoC, events |
| Auth | [`@czo/auth`](packages/modules/auth) | Authentication, permissions, apps |
| Stock Location | [`@czo/stock-location`](packages/modules/stock-location) | Physical inventory locations |

## Documentation

📖 **[docs.c-zo.dev](https://docs.c-zo.dev)** — Full documentation, API reference, and guides.

## Development

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `pnpm dev` | Run all apps |
| `pnpm dev:mazo` | Backend API only |
| `pnpm build` | Build all packages |
| `pnpm test` | Run tests |
| `pnpm lint` | Lint all packages |
| `pnpm check-types` | Type-check all packages |

## License

[MIT](LICENSE.md)
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add README.md
git commit -m "docs: rewrite root README with project overview and quick start"
```

More guides and modules coming soon.
